assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
The developer is creating a new Sentry organization on the free trial signup surface.
No Sentry organization, project, DSN, or SDK instrumentation exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Sentry account and project, instrument an application with a Sentry SDK, and open the first captured issue.

first_success.outcome: A new error appears in Issues and opens to show full details and context.
first_success.observable_signal: The Issues view shows a newly captured error with expandable event details.

route_selection.surface: Sentry Cloud signup and platform SDK quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete signup-to-first-issue route only after one platform SDK is fixed.

candidates.name: Node.js Error Monitoring quickstart
candidates.reason: Fully documented candidate, but the platforms index does not rank Node above peer SDKs.

candidates.name: Other first-class SDK families (Python, Java, .NET, Ruby, Go, mobile, and many JavaScript frameworks)
candidates.reason: Official Platforms docs present co-equal SDK routes with different packages, files, and verification steps.

candidates.name: Sentry REST API project management
candidates.reason: API manages resources; SDK quickstarts are the documented routes for sending application events.

required_path.action: Create a free Sentry account on the public signup form.

required_fields.label: Data Storage Location
required_fields.notes: US or EU; immutable after organization creation.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy agreement

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated Sentry organization.

required_path.action: Create a project and choose the application's platform.
required_path.observable_result: Sentry creates the project and shows platform configure guidance including a DSN.

required_path.action: Install and configure the selected Sentry SDK, trigger a first error, and open it in Issues.

required_fields.label: SDK install and instrumentation steps
required_fields.notes: Exact package, instrument file, preload/import order, and verification command depend on the unresolved platform choice.

required_path.observable_result: A new error appears in Issues with full event details.

prerequisites.description: A runnable application matching the eventually selected Sentry platform SDK.

external_gates.description: reCAPTCHA must be completed to submit signup.
Data storage location is immutable for the organization.

unavoidable_waits.description: Sentry must ingest and process the first error event before it appears in Issues.

platform_outcomes.description: Signup creates an organization on a 14-day Business trial that can later move to the free plan.
Project creation issues a DSN used by the SDK to associate events.

excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Optional Tracing, Profiling, or Logs product toggles beyond core Issues
excluded.reason: Error Monitoring/Issues is the documented first-success boundary for the create-project tutorial path.

excluded.item: Social signup via Google, GitHub, or Azure DevOps
excluded.reason: Peer auth alternatives to the observed email/password form; not required once email signup is available.

sources.title: Platforms
evidence_supported: Many co-equal official SDK families without a ranked default

sources.title: Sentry for Node.js
evidence_supported: Node install and instrument.js initialization pattern as one candidate route

uncertainties.question: Which Sentry SDK should represent the platform-wide shortest path to first Issues success?
uncertainties.impact: Package, runtime prerequisites, instrumentation files, event trigger, and verification steps all change by SDK.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision selecting one platform (or an official ranked default if one appears) and a complete cold-account walkthrough to the first Issues event.
